mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS levels, with a Rank IC below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, household income growth stagnates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price indices and retail sales indicates robust demand outpacing supply constraints, allowing firms to pass costs to consumers and sustain earnings growth, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL and RSAFS both rise, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and improving supply chain throughput, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts short-term equity valuations during periods of monetary accommodation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent, with a negative rank correlation coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with elevated housing starts reflects broad-based economic expansion and durable consumer spending, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in 60 percent of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0 percent and HOUST remains above 1000 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth signals tightening labor markets and rising wage pressures that initially boost consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS surges exceeding 500 thousand, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation alongside persistently low policy rates triggers expectations of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a positive hit rate exceeding 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment indicates improving labor productivity and easing supply bottlenecks, which stabilizes margins and supports positive monthly equity performance.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls, with an information coefficient below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.
